member.name: Brian Mathew
member.synopsis: Brian Mathew is the Liberal Democrat MP for Melksham and Devizes , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Genetics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential merits of a proactive approach to offering breast cancer gene (BRCA) testing to those who have lost a family member to ovarian or breast can
writtenQuestions.answerText: Genomic testing in the National Health Service in England is provided through the NHS Genomic Medicine Service and delivered by a national genomic testing network of seven NHS Genomic Laboratory Hubs (GLHs). The NHS GLHs deliver testing as directed by the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Genetics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will set out how his Department will roll out breast cancer gene (BRCA) testing nationally.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Genomic testing in the National Health Service in England is provided through the NHS Genomic Medicine Service and delivered by a national genomic testing network of seven NHS Genomic Laboratory Hubs (GLHs). The NHS GLHs deliver testing as directed by the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Genetics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to (a) define populations with high risk of carrying harmful breast cancer (BRCA) gene variants and (b) increase testing levels amongst those communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Genomic testing in the National Health Service in England is provided through the NHS Genomic Medicine Service and delivered by a national genomic testing network of seven NHS Genomic Laboratory Hubs (GLHs). The NHS GLHs deliver testing as directed by the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what additional resources are being given to the NHS Business Service Authority to clear the backlog of NHS Pension applications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department regularly meets with the NHS Business Services Authority (NHSBSA) to discuss performance levels. These discussions include the processing times for National Health Service pension applications.The NHSBSA aims to make the first payment for a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to support retiring NHS staff whose pensions are not processed within the target processing time.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department regularly meets with the NHS Business Services Authority (NHSBSA) to discuss performance levels. These discussions include the processing times for National Health Service pension applications.The NHSBSA aims to make the first payment for a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his department has have made of the effectiveness of the NHS Business Services Authority in processing NHS pension applications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department regularly meets with the NHS Business Services Authority (NHSBSA) to discuss performance levels. These discussions include the processing times for National Health Service pension applications.The NHSBSA aims to make the first payment for a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Syria: Politics and Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps she is taking to help support inclusive and representative governance in Syria and to help protect the rights of religious communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 22 June in response to Question 10866.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Glioblastoma: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that new treatment options for glioblastoma are reviewed and made available as quickly as possible.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that glioblastoma is a rare and aggressive brain tumour and is committed to improving outcomes for people affected by rare and less common cancers such as this.The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) makes re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Servants: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what the backlog is of Civil Service Pension Scheme cases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office awarded the contract to administer the Civil Service Pension Scheme to Capita in November 2023 under the previous government.The issues and delays facing a number of civil servants and pension scheme members in receiving their pension q...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Servants: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many civil servants are waiting for payment of a civil service pension in (a) Melksham and Devizes constituency and (b) the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office awarded the contract to administer the Civil Service Pension Scheme to Capita in November 2023 under the previous government.The issues and delays facing a number of civil servants and pension scheme members in receiving their pension q...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Integrated Care Boards: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of proposed reductions in funding for Integrated Care Boards on their ability to provide oversight of contracted services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are looking to reduce the cost of running integrated care boards (ICBs) to free up resources for frontline services. To support this, we have refocused their role on strategic commissioning and reduced their involvement in functions such as performance...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what mechanisms are available to remove an underperforming commissioned service provider before the expiry of a contract.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NHS Standard Contract allows a commissioner to terminate a contract either by issuing notice in accordance with the contract notice period, or in a number of other circumstances which include where a provider:is in persistent or repetitive breach of t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what mechanisms are in place to assess the performance of care providers which have been commissioned by the Integrated Care Board.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for assuring the quality, effectiveness, and value of the services they commission for their populations.ICBs assess provider performance through a range of mechanisms, including contract management arrangemen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Disability Living Allowance: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment his Department have made of the impact of delays to Disability Living Allowance decisions on families caring for children with hidden disabilities in (a) Melksham and Devizes and (b) the
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are committed to ensuring people can access financial support through Disability Living Allowance for children (DLAc) in a timely manner. Reducing customer journey times for our claimants is a priority for the Department and we are working to make impr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Disability Living Allowance: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to reduce the time taken for Disability Living Allowance Mandatory Reconsideration decisions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have approved overtime and reallocated decision makers to clear our outstanding Disability Living Allowance Child Mandatory Reconsideration cases more quickly. This will help us to reduce clearance times.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Medical Examinations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how his Department ensures individuals with (a) hidden and (b) fluctuating disabilities are properly assessed and receive adequate support.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Healthcare Professionals (HCPs) conducting the Work Capability Assessment and the Personal Independence Payment assessment are trained specialists in disability analysis. Their focus is on understanding the functional impact of a customer’s condition,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 21 May in response to Question 1592.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Voyeurism: Public Places
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps he is taking with the Secretary of State for the Home Department to help tackle the non-consensual filming of women.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Sadly, as technology becomes entwined with our day-to-day lives, we recognise the threat tech-enabled harm poses.That is why our VAWG Strategy sets out how we are seeking to tackle it.I am pleased to say on 1 April, we commenced the Protection from Sex-Ba...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Diethylstilbestrol
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of a public inquiry into the effects of diethylstilbestrol.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
